title: Chicken Karahi

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Karahi Recipe: Pakistani Classic at Home
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Pakistani cooking with our authentic Chicken Karahi recipe. Learn how to make this flavorful, restaurant-style dish at home with step-by-step instructions. Perfect for any occasion!
h2Headings: How to Make Chicken Karahi: Step-by-Step Instructions
Tips for the Perfect Chicken Karahi
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant plate of authentic Pakistani Chicken Karahi, garnished with fresh ginger and cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich, aromatic flavors of Pakistan with this homemade Chicken Karahi.

description: Chicken Karahi is a quintessential Pakistani dish, celebrated for its rich, spicy tomato-based gravy and tender chicken cooked in a traditional karahi wok. This flavorful curry is a staple in Pakistani households and restaurants, offering a delightful blend of aromatic spices. Its robust taste and simple preparation make it a beloved comfort food, perfect for sharing with family and friends.

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat oil in a large karahi or wok over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces and fry until they change color and are lightly browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics
instructions.text: Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and continue to fry for another 2-3 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Cook Tomatoes and Chillies
instructions.text: Add the chopped tomatoes and green chillies. Cover and cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes until the tomatoes soften and release their juices. Mash the tomatoes with the back of your spoon.

instructions.title: Season the Karahi
instructions.text: Add red chilli powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, black pepper powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil separates from the masala. If the mixture is too dry, add a splash of water.

instructions.title: Simmer and Tenderize
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to low, cover the karahi, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the chicken is fully cooked and tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Finishing Touches
instructions.text: Uncover, add garam masala powder, and mix gently. Garnish generously with julienned fresh ginger and chopped fresh coriander.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Serve your authentic Chicken Karahi immediately with hot naan, roti, or rice for a complete Pakistani meal.

tips: For a true Karahi flavor, use a heavy-bottomed karahi or wok. The high heat helps to sear the chicken and develop the rich, concentrated flavors.
Always use fresh, ripe tomatoes; they are crucial for the Karahi's signature tangy base. Avoid canned tomatoes for the most authentic taste.
Chicken Karahi is best served immediately, straight from the karahi. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days.
article: Chicken Karahi is a beloved Pakistani Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
